full_response:
Let the un-primed frame S be Alice’s rest frame.  
Metric: ds² = –dt² + dx² + dy² + dz²  (c = 1).  
Spatial compactification in S: 

(t ,x ,y ,z)  ∼  (t ,x+L ,y ,z).                                             (1)

Barbara moves with constant 3-velocity v = v êx in S, so her world-line is

x_B(t) = v t , y_B = z_B = 0 .                                               (2)

They depart together at t = 0, x = 0.  
Because of (1) the physical point x = L is the same as x = 0, so Alice and
Barbara meet again when Barbara has advanced one period in x:

t_A ≡ coordinate time of reunion in S = L / v .                             (3)

Proper time in Barbara’s own frame, however, must be computed directly in her
(rest) frame S′.

--------------------------------------------------
1.  Lorentz transform to Barbara’s frame
--------------------------------------------------
Choose S′ so that its origin coincides with theirs at departure and S′ moves
with speed v relative to S along +x.  Then

t′ = γ( t – v x) ,                                                          (4a)
x′ = γ( x – v t) ,                                                          (4b)
y′ = y , z′ = z ,   with γ = 1/√(1–v²).                                     (4c)

--------------------------------------------------
2.  How the identification looks in S′
--------------------------------------------------
Apply (4) to the identification vector (Δt = 0, Δx = L):

Δt′ = γ(0 – v L) = – γ v L ,                                                (5a)
Δx′ = γ(L – v·0) =   γ L .                                                  (5b)

Thus, in S′ the single spatial shift L is seen as a space–time shift

(Δt′ , Δx′) = ( –γ v L ,  γ L ).                                           (6)

--------------------------------------------------
3.  World-lines in S′
--------------------------------------------------
• Barbara (at rest):      x′_B = 0 .                                        (7)

• Alice:  x = 0  in S → in S′

x′_A(t′) = – v t′ .                                                        (8)

Because of the compactification, there are equivalent “image” world-lines of
Alice obtained by adding integer multiples n of (6):

t′_A(n) = t′ – n γ v L ,                                                    (9a)
x′_A(n) = – v t′ + n γ L .                                                  (9b)

--------------------------------------------------
4.  Reunion condition in S′
--------------------------------------------------
They meet when Barbara’s position x′_B = 0 coincides with some image of
Alice, i.e. when

x′_A(n) = 0  for some integer n.                                           (10)

Use (9b):

– v t′ + n γ L = 0  ⇒  t′ = n γ L / v .                                    (11)

But we must take the physical time coordinate of that same image, i.e. use
(9a):

t′_meet = t′ – n γ v L
         = n γ L / v  –  n γ v L
         = n γ L (1/v – v)
         = n γ L (1 – v²)/v
         = n L /(γ v).                                                     (12)

The first reunion corresponds to n = 1, so

Δτ_B   =   t′_meet   =   L /(γ v)                                          (13)
         =  (L / v) √{1 – v²} .                                            (14)

Since Barbara is at rest in S′, her proper time equals the coordinate time
t′_meet, and (14) exactly matches the invariant calculation in Alice’s
frame, confirming consistency.

--------------------------------------------------
5.  Result
--------------------------------------------------
In Barbara’s rest frame the elapsed proper time between departure and reunion is

\boxed{\displaystyle \Delta\tau_B = \frac{L}{\gamma v}
       = \frac{L}{v}\sqrt{1 - \frac{v^{2}}{c^{2}}}} 

(Setting c = 1 gives the first expression.)

--------------------------------------------------
Confidence: 88%
